entities.description: rockchip developer guide pcle cn is a document that provides developer guidance for pcie technology  specifically mentioning sections about controller initialization issues
pcle3 0             is a section in the document that discusses system anomalies during the initialization of pcie 3 0 controllers
pcle 2 0             is a section in the document that discusses system anomalies during the initialization of pcie 2 0 controllers
linux    is the kernel of the linux operating system  which is mentioned as needing to have pcie functionality disabled in device tree files

relations.description: rockchip developer guide pcle cn contains the section  pcle3 0              which discusses system anomalies during pcie 3 0 controller initialization
rockchip developer guide pcle cn contains the section  pcle 2 0              which discusses system anomalies during pcie 2 0 controller initialization
the section  pcle3 0              mentions that pcie functionality should be disabled in the linux kernel  device tree files  when not using pcie 2 0 3 0 features
the section  pcle 2 0              mentions that pcie functionality should be disabled in the linux kernel  device tree files  when not using pcie 2 0 3 0 features
the section  pcle3 0              specifies that pcie functionality should be disabled in device tree files when not using pcie 2 0 3 0 features
the section  pcle 2 0              specifies that pcie functionality should be disabled in device tree files when not using pcie 2 0 3 0 features
